title: A Heavenly Vision
speaker: Pastor Tito Lyro
source_files: 01-28-24 -PM- A Heavenly Vision - Revelation 5 - Pastor Tito Lyro.txt

outline.title: Introduction: John's Heavenly Vision
subpoints: Revelation 5 follows Revelation 4's vision of the throne room of God
The scroll in the right hand of God contains the destiny of the world — sealed with seven seals
The angel's challenge: who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?

outline.title: 1. God Holds the Destiny of the World in His Hand
subpoints: The scroll is in the Father's right hand — He governs all of history
The sealed scroll represents the unfolding of God's redemptive purposes for creation
This is a comfort: history is not spinning out of control but is in God's hand

outline.title: 2. The World Apart from Christ Is Utterly Hopeless
subpoints: No one in heaven, earth, or under the earth was found worthy to open the scroll
John wept greatly — he understood what this meant for the world
Every human system, philosophy, and religion fails to solve the problem of sin and death
Only one is worthy — and He is not from the creation but from God

outline.title: 3. The Slaughtered Lamb Reigns as Sovereign Lord
subpoints: The Lion of Judah = the Lamb who was slain — strength through sacrifice
Seven horns = perfect power; seven eyes = perfect wisdom and knowledge (the fullness of the Spirit)
Christ took the scroll — He is the rightful heir of all creation
The four living creatures and twenty-four elders fell down and worshipped
The new song: 'You are worthy' — worship flows from what Christ has done

outline.title: 4. The Atonement Is Gloriously Particular
subpoints: 'You purchased for God with Your blood men from every tribe, tongue, people, and nation'
The scope: every people group — missions is not optional but central to God's purpose
The certainty: those purchased will be there — Christ did not make salvation merely possible
The destiny: a kingdom and priests who will reign on the earth

outline.title: Application: Four Responses to This Vision
subpoints: Pray — bring before Christ the world He redeems
Love Christ supremely — He is worthy of more than we give Him
Be willing to die for the gospel — the martyrs under the altar testify to this
Bold witness — Christ's people from every nation will be gathered; we are instruments of that gathering

summary: Pastor Tito Lyro expounds Revelation 5, John's vision of the Lamb in the throne room of heaven. He draws out four great theological truths: God holds the destiny of the world in His hand (the scroll), the world apart from Christ is utterly hopeless (no created being worthy to open it), the slaughtered Lamb reigns as sovereign Lord, and the atonement is gloriously particular — purchasing men from every tribe and nation with certainty. He closes with four applications: pray, love Christ supremely, be willing to die for the gospel, and bear bold witness.
transcript:
## Introduction: A Vision of the Throne Room

In Revelation 4, John was caught up into the throne room of heaven and saw the awesome vision of God enthroned — the four living creatures, the twenty-four elders, the sea of glass, the flashes of lightning. In chapter 5, the scene continues, and our attention is drawn to a scroll.

> "And I saw in the right hand of Him who sat on the throne a scroll written inside and on the back, sealed with seven seals. Then I saw a strong angel proclaiming with a loud voice, 'Who is worthy to open the scroll and to loose its seals?'" (Revelation 5:1–2)

Four great truths are contained in this vision. I want to lay them before you.

## 1. God Holds the Destiny of the World in His Hand

The scroll is in the *right hand* of the One seated on the throne. This is the hand of power and authority. The scroll, written on both sides and sealed with seven seals, represents the complete and perfect decree of God for the whole of history — the unfolding of His redemptive purposes for creation.

This is one of the most comforting truths in Scripture. You may look at the world and see chaos, evil enthroned, war, injustice, suffering. But history is not spinning out of control. It is held in the right hand of the sovereign God. Every seal that is broken is broken by His authority and on His timetable.

## 2. The World Apart from Christ Is Utterly Hopeless

> "And no one in heaven or on the earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll, or to look at it. So I wept much, because no one was found worthy to open and read the scroll, or to look at it." (Revelation 5:3–4)

John wept *greatly* — the Greek is strong. He understood what this silence meant. If no one could open the scroll, the purposes of God for creation would remain sealed. History would have no resolution. The dead would not rise. Redemption would not come.

Every human solution — every philosophy, political system, religion, or ideology — stands before this challenge and falls silent. No man, no angel, no created being is worthy. If there is to be hope, it must come from outside the creation entirely.

## 3. The Slaughtered Lamb Reigns as Sovereign Lord

> "But one of the elders said to me, 'Do not weep. Behold, the Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has prevailed to open the scroll and to loose its seven seals.'" (Revelation 5:5)

And John turned to see the Lion — and saw a Lamb. A Lamb *as though it had been slain*. This is the great paradox of the gospel: the conquest was accomplished through sacrifice. The Lion conquers as the Lamb.

Seven horns: perfect power. Seven eyes: the seven spirits of God, perfect wisdom and knowledge — the fullness of the Holy Spirit. He came and took the scroll from the right hand of the Father — and the whole heaven erupted in worship.

> "You are worthy to take the scroll, and to open its seals; for You were slain, and have redeemed us to God by Your blood..." (Revelation 5:9)

Worship is always a response to what God has done. It is not manufactured by mood lighting or musical style. It flows from seeing clearly who Christ is and what He has accomplished.

## 4. The Atonement Is Gloriously Particular

> "...and have redeemed us to God by Your blood out of every tribe and tongue and people and nation..." (Revelation 5:9)

Notice: the Lamb *has* redeemed — past tense, accomplished. He did not make redemption merely possible. He actually purchased people — real people from every people group on earth.

This gives missions its foundation and its certainty. We are not going into the nations hoping that perhaps some will respond. We are going because Christ has purchased people from every tribe and tongue, and He will gather them. Our witness is the instrument He uses.

And the destiny of the redeemed: *a kingdom and priests to our God, and we shall reign on the earth*. The story does not end with souls floating in heaven. It ends with a renewed creation and redeemed humanity reigning with Christ.

## Application: Four Responses to This Vision

First, **pray**. Bring before the enthroned Christ the world He is redeeming. He is not indifferent to your prayers — He holds the destiny of the world.

Second, **love Christ supremely**. He is worthy — more worthy than our half-hearted devotion suggests. He who is worshipped by every angel and every creature deserves more than the scraps of our time and attention.

Third, **be willing to die for the gospel**. The martyrs appear later in Revelation — those who died for the testimony of Jesus. The Lamb who was slain calls us to take up our cross.

Fourth, **witness boldly**. People from your family, your neighborhood, your city, your nation are among the purchased. Go and tell them.
